title: Migration, Release Gates, Rollback, Recovery, Operations, and Defense · Runtime Lens: Release Gates
summary: The newest artifact replaces production without a route diff, removed URLs are discovered by readers, rollback requires rebuilding, caches preserve broken assets, ownership ends after deployment, and generated volume is called completion. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the release gates increment in production publication, migration, and recov…
objectives: Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
Compare old and new routes, redirects, canonicals, content identity, metadata, links, assets, accessibility, and reader tasks before cutover.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example publishing team handles case 8911 during a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness. A defensible solution applies Release Gates, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to production publication, migration, and recovery dossier.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-release-gates — Gate one immutable artifact on content approval, correctness, accessibility, security, performance, reproducibility, portability, licenses, and operational readiness.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-2
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-release-recovery-defense-2.
# Case 7417: a project-subpath GitHub Pages deployment.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def worked_static_site_release_recovery_defense_2(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "readerworked2",
        "source7417",
        "parse2dekrow",
        "routeworked27417",
        "artifact7417worked2",
        "recovery2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-release-gates-6w42mk
# Competency: ssg-release-gates.
# Case 8444: a large technical reference incremental rebuild.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic.
def evidence_ssg_release_gates_6w42mk(routes_before: int = 40, routes_after: int = 42, accounted_routes: int = 42, rollback_seconds: int = 90, recovery_owner: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_6w42mk = "6w42mk-km24w6-8444"
    assert evidence_variant_6w42mk
    evidence_axes_6w42mk = (
        "reader6w42mk",
        "source8444",
        "parsekm24w6",
        "route6w42mk8444",
        "artifact84446w42mk",
        "recoverykm24w68444",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_6w42mk)) == 6
    if routes_before < 1 or routes_after < 1:
        return False, routes_after, "migration-shape"
    if accounted_routes != routes_after:
        return False, accounted_routes, "route-diff-gap"
    if rollback_seconds < 0 or rollback_seconds > 300:
        return False, rollback_seconds, "rollback-budget"
    if not recovery_owner:
        return False, routes_after, "unowned-recovery"
    assert accounted_routes == routes_after
    return True, routes_after, "production-publication-defense"

steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Release Gates: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 7101 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to defend migration, immutable release, cache-aware rollback, recovery ownership, and residual risk; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
